critic_raw:
Preprocessing transformers are explicitly fit on the combined dataset before splitting ('fit on the combined dataset before any split occurs'). This applies to imputation statistics, scaling parameters, and text tokenizers. For a 5-year EHR dataset with vocabulary evolution in clinical notes, test-period token frequencies inform the training vocabulary — this is leakage that specifically advantages the clinical BERT model's preprocessing.

The feature sets are not comparable. The BioBERT model receives structured features AND full clinical text (discharge summaries, progress notes, medication lists). The logistic regression baseline receives only structured features — age, sex, Charlson index, admission type, LOS, diagnosis codes, insurance, prior readmission count. The design claims 'any performance difference is attributable to model architecture' but this is explicitly false: the models differ in both architecture and feature access. The observed improvement cannot be attributed to transformer architecture.

Out-of-system readmissions are acknowledged as a label quality issue, with the design noting sensitivity analysis assuming 'x% unobserved readmissions.' However, out-of-system readmission rates are typically 15-30% in regional hospital studies, and these are not randomly distributed — sicker patients with more complex care needs are more likely to seek care at academic centers outside the regional network. This creates systematic noise in the positive class that is correlated with case complexity, which is precisely the signal that text features capture. A BioBERT model trained on notes from complex patients who appear as false negatives may learn to systematically underpredict in this subpopulation.

The BioBERT model fine-tunes end-to-end with a 2-layer classification head, while the baseline is LR with L2 regularization. The BioBERT model has vastly more parameters and will have higher variance on a 50,000-patient dataset. No regularization strategy beyond dropout and weight decay is described. The risk of overfitting to training-period clinical documentation patterns is real and is not adequately controlled.
all_issues_raised: Preprocessing including imputation statistics, scaling, and tokenizers are fit on the combined dataset before splitting — test-period clinical note vocabulary and patient statistics inform training transformations.

BioBERT receives structured features plus full clinical text; logistic regression receives only structured features — the comparison confounds architecture with feature access, and the claim that any difference is 'attributable to model architecture' is explicitly incorrect.

Out-of-system readmissions (typically 15-30% of true readmissions in regional networks) are systematically correlated with case complexity — the positive class is enriched for simpler patients, and clinical text features that capture complexity will produce biased model behavior that is not captured by the sensitivity analysis.

BioBERT fine-tuned end-to-end has orders of magnitude more parameters than regularized LR — no analysis is provided for whether this capacity difference produces overfitting on the training period's documentation style that would not transfer to a prospective deployment setting.
all_issues_adjudicated: Preprocessing including imputation statistics, scaling, and tokenizers are fit on the combined dataset before splitting — test-period clinical note vocabulary and patient statistics inform training transformations.

BioBERT receives structured features plus full clinical text; logistic regression receives only structured features — the comparison confounds architecture with feature access, and the claim that any difference is 'attributable to model architecture' is explicitly incorrect.

Out-of-system readmissions (typically 15-30% of true readmissions in regional networks) are systematically correlated with case complexity — the positive class is enriched for simpler patients, and clinical text features that capture complexity will produce biased model behavior that is not captured by the sensitivity analysis.

BioBERT fine-tuned end-to-end has orders of magnitude more parameters than regularized LR — no analysis is provided for whether this capacity difference produces overfitting on the training period's documentation style that would not transfer to a prospective deployment setting.
